betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Tank Fluid I/O§f

§aConnect standard pipes and machines to your tanks!§f

The §9Pressure§f mod tanks expose a dedicated set of I/O blocks that act as fluid ports bridging the multiblock tank and any external pipe/conduit/machine network. These ports cleanly separate intake from output, support colored multi-channel routing, and can be combined to fully automate fluid distribution.

§6Tank Fluid Intake§f (pressure:tank_fluid_input)
Accepts fluids from §bstandard pipes§f from other mods. Each tank supports up to §e10 Fluid Intakes§f. By default, any connected pipe can push fluid in. Combine with a §bTank Fluid Filter§f to restrict intake to a single fluid type.

§6Tank Fluid Outlet§f (pressure:tank_fluid_output)
Pushes fluids into §bstandard pipes, machines, or tanks§f from other mods. Requires a suction source (a machine, a pull-pipe, or another active outlet) to drive the fluid out. Up to §e6 sides§f can be configured as separate colored outputs from the Tank GUI, each color routing into a different destination.

§6Powered Tank Fluid Outlet§f (pressure:tank_fluid_output_auto)
Like a standard Fluid Outlet, but §bautomatically pushes§f as much fluid as possible without needing an external suction source. Up to §e6 sides§f can be configured as separate colored outputs. Configurable from the Tank GUI.

§6Tank GUI Overview§f:
§b1.§f Fluid gauge — current level, hover for exact details.
§b2.§f Input slot — place containers to fill/empty the tank.
§b3.§f Output slot — processed containers appear here.
§b4.§f Filter slot — §9right-click with a fluid container§f to set the tank's accepted fluid filter.
§b5.§f Output slots — active output connections.
§b6.§f Output configuration — toggle each output mode and color.

§eTip§f: Use the colored output channels to sort different fluids into dedicated destinations without needing complex external pipe filtering.
